meta.title: Task Promise Spec
meta.summary: Canonical System Promise for the Task example.

intentPromises.name: TaskSystemIntent
intentPromises.statement: The task system must keep task lifecycle truth explicit, simple, and verifiable.
intentPromises.rationale: This system-level intent anchors the human purpose behind the Task Promise graph.

maps.note: Field truth is governed by the system-level intent.
Creation behavior is part of the system-level intent.
Completion behavior is part of the system-level intent.
Field invariants prove the system-level intent.

intentPromises.name: PreserveTaskLifecycleTruth
intentPromises.statement: A task's completion state must be explicit, reversible, and not duplicated through hidden flags.
intentPromises.rationale: Human intent should remain visible even after it is lowered into state fields, invariants, behaviors, and verification scenarios.

parents.note: Lifecycle truth is one branch of the system-level task intent.

maps.note: Task.status is the single business-state carrier.
The done state remains explicit and reversible.
Hidden duplicate completion flags are forbidden.
Completion behavior exists to fulfill lifecycle intent.
Invalid transitions must be proved, not assumed.

intentPromises.name: KeepTaskCreationSimple
intentPromises.statement: Creating a task should require only human-readable content and should start in todo state.
intentPromises.rationale: The create path should not ask callers to provide lifecycle state that the system can derive from the Promise.

parents.note: Creation simplicity is one branch of the system-level task intent.

maps.note: Title is the human-authored creation input.
A new task starts as todo with no completion timestamp.
Creation output must be covered by field invariant verification.

fieldPromises.name: TaskFieldPromise
fieldPromises.summary: Defines the Task object, its fields, valid states, and invariants.

fields.name: title
fields.semantic: Human-readable task content and must not be empty.

fields.name: completedAt
fields.semantic: Completion timestamp and only valid when status is done.

fields.name: createdAt
fields.semantic: Creation timestamp and immutable after creation.

fields.name: updatedAt
fields.semantic: Timestamp refreshed on every valid state or field mutation.

invariants.statement: When Task.status is done, Task.completedAt must exist.
When Task.status is todo, Task.completedAt must be null.
Task.createdAt cannot be changed by business actions.

globalConstraints.statement: A Task can only be in one business state at a time.
Every legal state transition must preserve field invariants.

forbiddenImplicitState.statement: Do not introduce isCompleted or any duplicate completion flag outside declared fields.
UI or cache-only markers must not become business truth for Task state.

functionPromises.name: CreateTaskFunctionPromise
functionPromises.summary: Creates a new task in todo state.
triggers: A user submits a create task request.

preconditions.statement: title must be present and non-empty.

successResults.statement: A successful create initializes a Task in todo state with null completedAt.

failureConditions.statement: The action must reject when title is empty.

forbidden.statement: CreateTask must not create a task directly in done state.
CreateTask must not write undeclared fields.

functionPromises.idempotency: Repeated create requests are not idempotent unless an external deduplication key is defined outside this Promise.

functionPromises.name: CompleteTaskFunctionPromise
functionPromises.summary: Marks an existing todo task as done.
triggers: A user requests to complete an existing task.

preconditions.statement: The target Task must currently be in todo state.

successResults.statement: A successful completion sets status to done and writes completedAt.

failureConditions.statement: The action must reject if the task does not exist.
The action must reject or return no-op when the task is already done.

forbidden.statement: CompleteTask must not update status without updating completedAt.
CompleteTask must not modify id, title, or createdAt.

functionPromises.idempotency: A second completion attempt on a done task must be rejected or explicitly treated as a no-op.

verificationPromises.name: TaskFieldInvariantVerification
verificationPromises.claim: Task field invariants always hold after legal state changes.

scenarios.name: CreateTask creates a todo task with null completedAt
then: The returned task has status todo.
The returned task has completedAt set to null.
The returned task has createdAt and updatedAt values.
regressionGuards: Do not allow default creation in done state.

scenarios.name: CompleteTask preserves done invariants
given: A Task exists in todo state.
then: The task completedAt is not null.
The task updatedAt changes while createdAt remains unchanged.
regressionGuards: Do not permit status changes without completedAt.

evidenceRequired: Assertions over the returned or persisted Task object.
A test trace that shows createdAt remains unchanged on completion.

verificationPromises.name: TaskTransitionRejectionVerification
verificationPromises.claim: Invalid transitions are rejected without inventing hidden state.

scenarios.name: CompleteTask rejects an already done task
given: A Task exists in done state.
then: The action is rejected or returns an explicit no-op.
No additional business state is introduced.
regressionGuards: Do not write a second completion marker.
Do not introduce helper flags such as isCompleted.

evidenceRequired: Execution output or assertions showing no hidden state is persisted.
failureCriteria: A repeated completion creates a new business state.
A repeated completion writes undeclared fields.
